steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The researchers concluded that neither species is drawn to a certain type of area. The finding driving that conclusion is the one shared preference: both species require standing water for more than 60 days per year. The blank must describe the opposite of that shared condition.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'there is standing water for far fewer than 60 days per year.': This is the direct opposite of the shared 60-day requirement, so it logically explains why neither species would be drawn there. ✓
  • Choice B — 'species with breeding seasons longer than 60 days are likely to be present.': Breeding-season length of other species is never mentioned as a factor either heron cares about. ✗
  • Choice C — 'there are any features that attract the other species.': The passage shows the two herons share one preference but differ on water depth, so features that attract one do not necessarily repel the other. ✗
  • Choice D — 'there is relatively deep water during the breeding season.': Only small white herons prefer deep water; small dark herons do not, so this is not a shared deterrent for both. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
